title: Fix owner privilege loss — harden stable owner principal and role checks
projectName: Reliable Home Appliance Repair Service Desk

requirements.text: In backend/main.mo, declare the owner principal as a stable let constant (not a mutable stable var) initialized with Principal.fromText("q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe"). This value must never be overwritten by any upgrade, migration, or role-management call. Remove any stable var or TrieMap entry that could shadow or replace the owner principal.

quotes: Let's go back to version 8, troubleshoot why I lost my owner privileges.
Owner loses privileges after certain builds — root cause: possible overwrite of stable owner principal or mismatch between NNS principal and app-derived principal.

acceptanceCriteria: The owner principal 'q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe' is declared as a stable immutable value (stable let or equivalent) that cannot be overwritten at runtime or during canister upgrades.
No role-management function (addRole, removeRole, setRole, or equivalent) can alter the owner principal.
The roles TrieMap does not contain an entry for the owner principal — ownership is determined solely by direct equality comparison against the stable owner constant.
After a canister upgrade, the same principal still passes the #owner permission check without any additional configuration.

requirements.text: In backend/main.mo, rewrite the hasPermission helper so that #owner checks are a direct equality comparison between the caller and the stable owner constant (REQ-48) and never fall through to the roles TrieMap. The roles TrieMap is only consulted for #authorized and #admin role checks. Ensure no code path can grant #owner via the TrieMap.

quotes: owner check must be a direct equality comparison against this stable var so it cannot be overwritten

acceptanceCriteria: hasPermission(caller, #owner) returns true if and only if caller == ownerPrincipal (the stable constant).
hasPermission(caller, #authorized) returns true for the owner principal and for any principal assigned #authorized or #admin in the TrieMap.
Assigning #authorized to any principal via addAuthorized does not grant #owner privileges.
Removing a principal from the TrieMap does not affect the owner's permissions.

requirements.text: Audit and fix every owner-guarded backend endpoint (setStripeKey, getStripeKey, addAuthorizedPrincipal, removeAuthorizedPrincipal, listAuthorizedPrincipals, createLaborRate, updateLaborRate, deleteLaborRate, createPackage, updatePackage, deletePackage, and any other #owner-only function) to use the hardened hasPermission check from REQ-49. Ensure none of these functions accept the roles TrieMap as a fallback for the owner check.

quotes: I need multiple users to be able to have full crud abilities
troubleshoot why I lost my owner privileges

acceptanceCriteria: Calling any owner-only endpoint from a non-owner principal returns an #unauthorized or equivalent error.
Calling any owner-only endpoint from the owner principal (q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe) succeeds.
No canister upgrade resets these guards.

requirements.text: Ensure all standard CRUD endpoints (createClient, updateClient, deleteClient, createJob, updateJob, deleteJob, updateJobStatus, createPart, updatePart, deletePart, usePartOnJob, addJobPhoto, removeJobPhoto, addLaborLineItem, removeLaborLineItem, updateJobSchedule, updateJobPayment, createPaymentIntent, storeUserSignature) accept calls from any principal that passes hasPermission(caller, #authorized) — which includes both the owner principal and any TrieMap-registered #authorized principal. This supports multiple technician/admin users with full CRUD access.

quotes: I need multiple users to be able to have full crud abilities

acceptanceCriteria: A principal registered as #authorized in the TrieMap can successfully call all standard CRUD endpoints.
The owner principal can also call all standard CRUD endpoints.
An unauthenticated or unregistered principal is rejected from all CRUD endpoints.
Multiple #authorized principals can be registered simultaneously and all retain CRUD access.

requirements.text: In the frontend Settings page (SettingsPage.tsx), update the owner-check logic that gates the Settings screen. The frontend isOwner check must compare the authenticated Internet Identity principal against the hardcoded string 'q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe'. Do not rely solely on a backend role query that could return a stale or missing result. If the backend getMyRole (or equivalent) query is used, also verify locally. The Settings screen must render fully for the owner principal.

quotes: Version 8 was so close to being perfect

acceptanceCriteria: When logged in as q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe, the Settings screen renders all owner-only sections (labor rates, Stripe key, maintenance packages, role management).
When logged in as any other principal, the Settings screen either shows a restricted message or redirects.
The Dashboard owner-only metrics card and Labor Rates card render for the owner principal.
No page shows 'Metrics restricted' when the owner is logged in (as visible in the uploaded screenshots).

requirements.text: Add a migration guard in backend/main.mo (or migration.mo if needed) to preserve the stable owner principal across all future upgrades. On upgrade, if any logic would reset or overwrite the ownerPrincipal value, abort or skip that step. Document the stable owner principal in a comment so future builds do not accidentally replace it.

quotes: Owner loses privileges after certain builds — root cause: possible overwrite of stable owner principal

acceptanceCriteria: After deploying a new version of the canister, the owner principal remains q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe.
The stable owner value is clearly commented in the source so it is not accidentally changed in future builds.
No migration logic resets the owner to a default or anonymous principal.

constraints: The owner principal must always be q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe — do not change this value.
Do not remove or break any existing CRUD endpoints, role management UI, or previously implemented features.
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Do not add new features beyond fixing owner privilege loss and hardening role checks.
Do not integrate a new authentication system — Internet Identity remains the only auth mechanism.
Do not change the Stripe integration, invoice, calendar, or routing screens as part of this fix.
